evals.prompt: I need to create a CoApi client for the JSONPlaceholder API (https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com). It should have endpoints to get all posts, get a post by ID, and create a new post. I want to use the reactive programming model with Flux/Mono. Generate the interface and data classes, plus the application.yml config.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin interface with @CoApi annotation, @HttpExchange methods for GET /posts, GET /posts/{id}, POST /posts, data classes for Post, and application.yml with the base URL config.

evals.prompt: I have a microservice architecture with a user-service registered in Consul. I want to create a CoApi client that uses Spring Cloud LoadBalancer to call user-service. The client should have endpoints for getUser(id) and listUsers(). Show me the full setup including dependencies, the client interface, and the application.yml configuration for both reactive and sync modes.
evals.expected_output: Gradle dependencies (coapi-spring-boot-starter, spring-cloud-starter-loadbalancer), @CoApi interface with serviceId, application.yml with coapi.mode and per-client filter/interceptor config.

evals.prompt: I'm writing tests for my CoApi client that calls an external weather API. I want to use ApplicationContextRunner to verify the client bean is registered correctly, and also write a Spring Boot integration test that actually calls the API. Show me both test patterns using the project's testing conventions (fluent-assert, JUnit 5).
evals.expected_output: Two test classes: one using ApplicationContextRunner for unit-level bean verification, one using @SpringBootTest for integration testing. Both should follow the project's conventions with fluent-assert (.assert()) and proper test structure.

evals.prompt: My CoApi client interface is annotated with @CoApi, but Spring fails at startup with NoSuchBeanDefinitionException for that interface. Give me a focused troubleshooting checklist.
evals.expected_output: A checklist that verifies the target is an interface, the interface is annotated or registered through @EnableCoApi, scanning or coapi.base-packages covers the package, the starter dependency is present, and external JAR clients are registered explicitly.

evals.prompt: Explain CoApi base URL precedence when @CoApi(baseUrl), @CoApi(serviceId), and coapi.clients.<name>.base-url are involved.
evals.expected_output: An explanation that separates annotation definition resolution from HTTP client factory resolution: @CoApi(baseUrl) wins over serviceId inside CoApiDefinition, serviceId becomes lb://<serviceId> when baseUrl is blank, and coapi.clients.<name>.base-url overrides the parsed definition when the HTTP client factory builds the client.
